frameworks.name: resting-state functional connectivity
frameworks.one_line: inter-regional correlations of spontaneous BOLD fluctuations as a brain-state signature

frameworks.name: locus ceruleus-norepinephrine neuromodulation
frameworks.one_line: catecholaminergic system as dynamic sculptor of cortical interaction topography

frameworks.name: structural-functional connectivity mapping
frameworks.one_line: fixed anatomy sets a baseline that state-dependent factors modulate

frameworks.name: resting-state networks
frameworks.one_line: canonical large-scale network partition used to evaluate modulation specificity

core_question: Does endogenous catecholaminergic neuromodulation dynamically shape the topography of intrinsic functional connectivity in the human brain?
key_assumptions: spontaneous BOLD correlations index inter-regional functional interactions
anatomical connectivity partly determines but does not fully fix intrinsic topography
atomoxetine elevates synaptic catecholamine levels via NE transporter blockade
manipulations: pharmacological catecholamine elevation via atomoxetine vs placebo
analysis across three levels of spatial organization
key_findings: atomoxetine reduced inter-regional correlation strength across spatial scales
decrease followed an anterior-posterior cortical gradient
magnitude of decrease depended on baseline functional connectivity strength
effect strongest for between-network connections relative to within-network
interpretations: catecholamines reduce the strength of functional interactions during rest
neuromodulation imparts spatial specificity to state-dependent connectivity changes
